does: This paper presents a collection of incremental updates to the circuits research agenda, covering three main areas: improvements to jailbreak understanding through circuit analysis, advances in identifying dense features within neural networks, and foundational work for scaling up interpretability research. The authors demonstrate concrete applications of mechanistic interpretability to understand adversarial vulnerabilities in language models, develop techniques for discovering interpretable feature representations beyond sparse autoencoders, and establish infrastructure for conducting circuit-level analysis on larger models. Each update builds on existing mechanistic interpretability foundations with empirical results showing how circuit techniques can be applied to practical safety concerns like jailbreaks and feature discovery.
connects: This work extends the broader circuits research program initiated by prior work on mechanistic interpretability and neural network decomposition. It builds on existing frameworks for understanding how specific computations emerge in transformer models, connects to recent research on sparse autoencoders and feature extraction, and responds to growing interest in applying interpretability tools to adversarial robustness and safety. The jailbreak analysis extends prior work analyzing model vulnerabilities through circuit inspection, while the dense features work builds on and potentially addresses limitations in sparse feature discovery methods. These updates integrate insights from mechanistic interpretability, adversarial machine learning, and scalable interpretability to address both theoretical understanding and practical safety applications.
enables: These updates position the circuits research agenda to tackle increasingly complex questions about model behavior and safety. The jailbreak insights could inform better defenses against prompt injection attacks by revealing precise computational vulnerabilities. The dense feature discovery methods open pathways toward more complete feature catalogs that capture the full representational capacity of neural networks beyond sparse decompositions. The infrastructure development for interpretability at scale creates foundation for understanding larger, more capable models where safety concerns become more acute. Together, these advances suggest future work could combine circuit analysis with adversarial robustness, scale interpretability to frontier models, and develop principled interpretability-based alignment techniques grounded in mechanistic understanding rather than behavioral observation.
